Our Story

We’ve got the perfect place to start this shit— the actual beginning.

Photo

Our founders Jack and Dorian are literal day ones. They were born on the same day, in the same year, in the same city! Safe to say life had them on the same path from the start.

Fast forward a few years and the two met in the back of a high school classroom. They quickly found out that they had a lot more in common than their birthdays and bonded over their shared love of MMA and similar entrepreneurial desires.

BAM first got its start over a summer weekend after a bit of weed and a lot of locking in. Fueled by their intense ambition, strong passion, and an unhealthy amount of Red Bull, the two friends quickly pushed their dream out of a Racine basement and into the successful management firm it is today.

I guess you could call this whole thing a classic college startup, but we personally think it’s more fun to see it as something bigger. It’s a manifestation of the American Dream, something that’ll forever change the future MMA, yada yada yada…

Photo

Go for what you want and have a lot of fucking fun doing it.

Meet the Team

Jack Rosenthal
Founder & Head of Management

Jack Rosenthal has always had an inclination for entrepreneurship and left high school early to develop his skills in business. Before entering the world of MMA, he held stints as a Christmas tree salesman, celebrity bodyguard, school mascot, and parade balloon handler. Jack became a credentialed journalist at 16 and has since covered some of the most exclusive events on the planet. He plans on becoming an Army combat correspondent.

Dorian Schneider
Co-Founder & Director of Talent

Dorian Schneider gave up school to pursue his love of MMA full time, moving alone to Wisconsin to train with the famed Ben Rothwell. He is very passionate about the craft of fighting and started competing in tournaments after just four weeks. In addition to Ben Rothwell, he has also trained with people like Beneil Dariush. It is Dorian’s goal to enter the United Fighting League (UFC).

Zayn Ali is currently studying computational mathematics at Penn State and has a strong interest in business, technology, and media. He brings a problem-solving mindset to BAM and enjoys helping build ideas into something organized and real. With experience in robotics, Scouting, and technical work, Zayn is excited to contribute to BAM as the company grows in the combat sports space.

Samuel Brothers has always enjoyed being involved in his community and helping others. Over the years he has volunteered on many committees and even served as chairman for several of them. He has accumulated more than 200 volunteer hours and is currently a student at Western Kentucky University studying archaeology.

Jerome Comar is a Harvard College sophomore dual concentrating in physics and earth and planetary sciences. He is a co-founder of the Harvard College Concord Institute. He is a natural born entrepreneur and has a business acumen that is second to none.

Marshall has always been an extroverted person. He loves being around people and learning new things. He pursued many clubs in high school to grow this passion. He loves to be able to work with people on certain situations/problems to find the best solution. Being able to learn how to work around what everyone wants is something he enjoys greatly. Marshall is currently studying Mechanical Engineering Technology and hopes to get his MBA after some time in industry.

Biography will appear here once added.

BAM's…

BOARD OF ADVISORS

We’ve brought together a great group of people from the sports and entertainment world to help guide Boxing Access Media as we grow. Our advisors have years of experience in combat sports, media, athlete management, and business, giving us valuable knowledge and support as we continue to build and grow within the sport.

Ben “Big Ben” Rothwell
Board Member & Partner

Ben Rothwell is one of the most accomplished heavyweight fighters in MMA history. With a professional record of 39–14 and 28 knockouts, Rothwell competed in the UFC for over a decade, defeating top heavyweights including Alistair Overeem, Josh Barnett, and Matt Mitrione. In 2025, he captured the BKFC Heavyweight Championship at KnuckleMania 5. Off the canvas, Rothwell owns and operates Rothwell MMA in Kenosha — a premier martial arts academy that has trained fighters at every level since 2011 and is where several BAM roster members hone their craft.

rothwellmma.com ↗
